title: armeria humilis subsp. odorata
intro: Armeria humilis subsp. odorata, commonly known as the sweet thrift, is a plant species within the genus Armeria, characterized by its pink or white flowers and low-growing habit. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, sweet thrift is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including relief from colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as being used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Sweet thrift can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
